transcript:
Nick Ducoff: So, thank you all for being here. I'm really excited to talk to Sergio about all things stablecoins. So Anchorage, of course, is known for custody, but they're much more than that. Sergio, why don't you start by sharing a little bit about what Anchorage is up to?

Sergio Mello: Anchorage sits exactly in the middle between traditional finance and decentralized finance. Our founders, eight years ago, decided that the only way to bring institutions on-chain was to provide a safe environment for their custody of digital assets. So Anchorage Digital started with custody in mind, developed with more line of businesses with trading and staking and settlements, obtained a federal banking license in the US, which is a big deal for a crypto company. And it's also a big deal for a federally licensed bank to be doing crypto natively in the US. And more recently, we also expanded into stablecoin as a business. So we launched stablecoin as a service. Our first clients are Tether with USAT, Ethena with USDTb, Western Union with USDPT, which is on Solana first. We're going to be another announcement later today. And we are also expanding the stablecoin business as a perfect nexus to connect existing banks and traditional institutions to traditional finance and to bring their dollars on blockchain to start using blockchain as a primary rail for their growth and expansions.

Nick Ducoff: So what exactly does stablecoin as a service mean?

Sergio Mello: Stablecoin, first of all, are a beautiful instrument. They combine a payment rail with a treasury instrument with a payment token all together. So it's, from the regulatory standpoint, it took a while before regulators started to understand it and really nail the path, which I think now we're on to. But from a user perspective, the beauty is that they can serve use cases for retail, for small businesses, and for the largest asset managers all at once. And so at Anchorage, what we want to do is to provide the full platform, the full stack can serve these use cases. Specifically for institutions, because Anchorage Digital is an institutional-only platform, we allow settlements, cross-border, cross-chain, fiat to crypto, crypto to fiat. And this is really where the rubber hits the road. Most of the inbound that we have from banks that are curious about stablecoins have to understand this friction point first: how do I get on-chain? We will see a future in the next five years that will be very different from the previous five years of stablecoins where most of the use cases were centered around arbitrage between pairs in different exchanges or dollar accounts for those who couldn't really be banked properly in their countries. Whereas in the next five years, we're going to see, I say tens of trillions, and I'm not trying to be exaggerated here, we'll see tens of trillions of dollars of money supply, the broad money, the M2 that now is in bank deposits, will be shifting on-chain, on Solana. And that's where we need to provide the infrastructure for those banks to have the comfort to keep those dollars on-chain. So the problem of the friction between fiat and chain will be less and less felt, and the dollar and the currencies will keep staying on-chain.

Nick Ducoff: So to get to tens of trillions, obviously you need distribution. A couple talks before ours, Kash from Jupiter was here. JupUSD has backing by USDTb, one of Ethena's products. Can you maybe talk about that specific example, how it works?

Sergio Mello: Yes, absolutely. If you look at the building blocks, the first building block is issuance. So the minting of a dollar on-chain. Anchorage Digital is the only federally regulated stablecoin issuer as of today. We do it under our federal bank charter. This is even pre-Genius, even before the effective rulemaking of the Genius law. Over that building block, the USDTb stablecoin that will be minted on Solana as of next week or the week after, allows for composable utility. And that's really the beauty of bringing dollars on-chain, is that you can spin up applications, extensions, and utility with a few lines of code. So the great example here is that Jupiter can take a building block like USDTb, wrap it into their ecosystem, and provide different type of utilities in their ecosystem using the hard work that Anchorage has done from the compliance and issuance perspective, that Ethena has done in the knowledge of distributing and creating liquidity and an environment for a stablecoin, and further adding the application layer for retail businesses and financial institutions to use it on-chain.

Nick Ducoff: And what's the backing for USDTb?

Sergio Mello: USDTb is Genius ready. So the five or six or seven allowed asset classes that are into a fiat-backed stablecoins in the US. USDTb specifically is backed by BUIDL, BUIDL on Solana.

Nick Ducoff: Which is BlackRock's money fund.

Sergio Mello: BUIDL is a Genius compliant money market fund, so short-term treasuries mostly, issued by BlackRock and Securitize.

Nick Ducoff: So JupUSD is effectively backed by BlackRock's BUIDL money fund.

Sergio Mello: That's exactly the full composition of the stack. Yeah.

Nick Ducoff: So you talked about Genius Act and the importance of regulatory clarity to help propel stablecoins forward. Can you talk a little bit about just how important the passing of Genius was and maybe compare Genius with MiCA in Europe?

Sergio Mello: Yeah, so anecdotally, I was the one making the calls and tried to convince people to do stablecoins until July this year. And then from July to now, I'm the one who has to receive the calls to explain how to come do your stablecoin. This is pretty obvious. Strategically speaking, Genius is probably the best thing the US government has done in terms of power projection and assertion of their USD monetary policy globally. The results are immediate and measurable. The fact that Tether, which we all know here is the largest on-chain dollars distributor in the world, immediately decided to on-shore part of their activity and launch the USAT stablecoin issued by Anchorage is really the tip of the iceberg. I can tell you that the demand for Genius ready or Genius compliant stablecoins is enormous. We receive a lot of inbounds from all sort of institutions and distributors and retailers globally that understand the importance of the transformation the blockchain allows in enabling existing non-finance business to have a stake in an otherwise unapproachable industry like global money movement.

Nick Ducoff: So you talked about how you were making the calls and then you started receiving them. I think, you know, we've felt some of that pull too. At this year's Money 20/20 conference in Las Vegas, the CEO of Western Union announced the Western Union stablecoin that's being built on Solana rails. And he said when they were evaluating chains to build on, they chose Solana because it was the institutional grade chain purpose-built for a use case like Western Union. Can you talk a little bit more about why businesses are choosing to issue stablecoins on Solana?

Sergio Mello: This is a story of two flavors, I think. There's the technical flavor, and of course Solana has proven to be institutional ready with a set of features that enable programmable privacy, programmable features into token and token transfers. That's essential. It's really the foundation. But there's also a story of advocacy and business development. And that's, we, people that have been trying to bring institutions on-chain for half a decade, know how hard it was in the past. And having done all that groundwork to educate and advocate for bringing finance on-chain for the past years is really what's now converting into those institutions having built the trust to come and build on Solana first. So we do see, because of this hard work by the Solana Foundation, we can measurably see how institutions that now come to Anchorage for infrastructure already have a choice for blockchain, and that is Solana.

Nick Ducoff: So Western Union, of course, has users all over the world. Stablecoins today have primarily been US dollar denominated. What do you see as the future in terms of currency pairs? Will USD stablecoins continue to dominate or will we start to see stablecoins in local fiat from around the world?

Sergio Mello: Yeah, that ties back to my previous assertion about the first phase and the second phase of the stablecoin eras. In the past, stablecoins had a different utility. Today, they really start to be part of sovereign monetary policy. And it is obvious that a sovereign state would not have control over its monetary policy with full dollarization of the economy. So it is obvious that the major economies in the world that can have control over their sovereign states will have proper stablecoin legislation that will allow their local currencies to be competitive. This is really a story of missing out. Any sovereign state and regulator that does not come together in regulating stablecoins within the next six to 12 months will be literally left out. I could probably compare it to the equivalent of not having your phone system connected to the rest of the world or not having the underground internet cables to connect to the rest of the internet of the world. It's as bad. So we are seeing the regulators making moves. ADGM has made great moves. The Central Bank of the UAE is very far ahead of many other states. But this is a word of advice to the other regulators globally that if they don't catch up, they're really going to miss out on their country. So to answer your question, the effect on on-chain currencies is that we will see dozens, probably hundreds of non-USD currency stablecoins. And there will be a very developed market for on-chain FX very soon. I think sooner than we expect. All these phenomenons are exponential. They are slow until they hit the hockey stick moment and then it happens very fast. I can see by the end of next year already having some mature market for some currency pairs on-chain and by the end of 2027 to have a solid alternative to the exotic FX market. Let's remember that stablecoins are beautiful, and I will say this many times today, stablecoins are beautiful, I'm the Head of Stablecoins at Anchorage so clearly I have to also defend my job here, stablecoins are beautiful because they settle instantly in transfer. So like any token transfer on-chain, the transfer equals settlement. The biggest problem in the FX market today, that has been probably the same for I don't know how many centuries, is that only few currencies, the hard currencies, are traded with a system organized by CLS that allows for quick settlement. But most of the exotic currencies instead lag behind with actual settlement by a couple of days. And so the spreads are big, the cost is big, the counterparty risk is big, and someone has to take the risk means cost. The ability to do those exotic currency transfers on-chain means that the settlement risk goes away. And so it's a huge improvement to the global financial system. Therefore, it will make foreign currencies actually more powerful and useful.

Nick Ducoff: And what do you think needs to be true for on-chain trading venues to win the FX market once it comes on-chain?

Sergio Mello: This is a story of composability. So as we saw from the keynotes just before this one, adding an app, a feature, a utility on-chain, it's just a matter of a few lines of code and some developers and entrepreneurs that have a vision and want to execute it on-chain. So the sky is the limit really. This is like an early internet moment where you ask, what is the next website going to do? Everything.

Nick Ducoff: So it's easy to kind of say, okay, in a couple years we're going to have 10 trillion dollars of stablecoins, and I do, like you, believe that. What does the world look like in six months? So a little bit out in the future, does it look materially different than it looks today?

Sergio Mello: In six months, we will see mostly the regulatory landscape catching up quickly. Genius rulemaking, so the Treasury and OCC, the bank regulator in the US, should finish their rulemaking for stablecoins in the course of the next year. Foreign regulators will start aligning very quickly and seeking reciprocity. Some jurisdictions like Europe hopefully will change their mind about considering stablecoins a second-class citizen in the financial system. And we will have that level of clarity that will make this conversation no longer a question on if, but when. It's really going to happen. And it's only towards the end of next year that we will see the real institutional adoption. And that's where we talk trillions and no longer hundreds of billions.

Nick Ducoff: So Solana stablecoins have grown this year from four some-odd billion to 16 some-odd billion. Where do you think Solana stablecoins are at the end of next year?

Sergio Mello: Trillions.

Nick Ducoff: Trillions. All right, you heard it first. Trillions on Solana in stablecoins at the end of next year. Sergio, thank you so much for joining us today.

Sergio Mello: Thanks for having me here. Thank you.

# Anchorage Digital Becomes Preferred Custodian for Stable’s Mainnet Launch
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64b0dcb6fecf02d30d8dd905/6913be7ed0cd17aa23a35565_webflow-920x495-stable%402x.png)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64b0dcb6fecf02d30d8dd905/64c4e75a8c52bbd10a154d02_author-image.jpg)
Anchorage Digital
Author
Nov 12, 2025
[
](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=&title=&summary=&source=)
[
](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?)
[
](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=&t=)
Anchorage Digital, the leading crypto platform for investors and innovators, is selected to serve as a preferred custodian for Stable’s mainnet launch, providing custody support for its native STABLE token and USDT0, a bridged version of Tether’s USDT stablecoin, on day one.
As Stable prepares to go live, we're excited to offer secure custody for these assets through Anchorage Digital Bank and Anchorage Digital Singapore. Self-custody through Porto, our institutional-grade wallet, will also be available. Staking support for STABLE will follow soon.
Stable is a blockchain designed to bring scalable, stablecoin-native infrastructure to global markets. It enables onchain settlement, payments, and liquidity built directly around stablecoins.
Anchorage Digital has worked with the Stable team from early stages as they prepare to go to mainnet. With this partnership, institutional participants will have a seamless entry point to custody and transact within the Stable ecosystem.
*“Our mission is to support protocols from their earliest stages and enable them to scale securely with institutional-grade infrastructure. Stable represents a new chapter for stablecoin-native networks, and we’re proud to be a day-one partner as it works towards mainnet.”****
- Nathan McCauley, Co-Founder and CEO of Anchorage Digital***
*“We’re excited to deepen our partnership with Anchorage Digital, a trusted institutional custodian that has been with us since the beginning. Their regulatory expertise and robust technical strength creates a solid foundation to prepare for our mainnet launch. Together, we’re not just advancing a more secure and scalable stablecoin ecosystem; we’re setting the standard for the future of on-chain institutional-grade financial infrastructure.”****
- Brian Mehler, CEO of Stable***
Anchorage Digital remains committed to expanding access to stablecoin ecosystems, and supporting protocols that advance the future of onchain financial infrastructure.[Get in touch](https://www.anchorage.com/get-in-touch)with our team if you’re interested in STABLE custody.[Learn more](https://www.anchorage.com/who-we-serve/protocols)about our comprehensive offerings for protocols.
About Anchorage Digital‍
Anchorage Digital is a global crypto platform that enables institutions to participate in digital assets through trading, staking, custody, governance, settlement, stablecoin issuance, and the industry’s leading security infrastructure. Home to Anchorage Digital Bank N.A., the only federally chartered crypto bank in the U.S., Anchorage Digital also serves institutions through Anchorage Digital Singapore, which is licensed by the Monetary Authority of Singapore; Anchorage Digital NY, which holds a BitLicense from the New York Department of Financial Services; and self-custody wallet Porto by Anchorage Digital. # Anchorage Digital Offers Rewards on USDtb and USDe
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64b0dcb6fecf02d30d8dd905/6925d413fa2b5bb5af554e47_webflow-920x495-usdtb%402x%20(1).png)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64b0dcb6fecf02d30d8dd905/64c4e75a8c52bbd10a154d02_author-image.jpg)
Anchorage Digital
Author
Nov 25, 2025
[
](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=&title=&summary=&source=)
[
](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?)
[
](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=&t=)
Anchorage Digital Bank is expanding access to**USDtb**, America’s first federally regulated stablecoin. Following a partnership with Ethena Labs, Anchorage Digital is now the sole issuer of USDtb. Clients can now mint, redeem, and collect rewards for holding USDtb on Anchorage Digital’s platform. Clients can also collect rewards for holding**USDe**, the synthetic dollar created by Ethena Labs.
‍*“We’re setting the standard for how digital dollars can operate in a transparent and compliant way within the U.S. financial system. The launch of America’s first federally regulated stablecoin was a major milestone in building a trusted foundation. Now, with USDtb minting, redemptions, and stablecoin rewards available through Anchorage Digital, we’re accelerating the adoption of safe and trusted stablecoins at global scale*.”***
- Nathan McCauley, CEO and Co-Founder, Anchorage Digital***
**About USDtb and USDe**
* **USDtb:**America’s first federally regulated stablecoin, issued by Anchorage Digital Bank. USDtb is a stablecoin backed by high quality short duration treasury assets including BlackRock's BUIDL fund. Created with institutional adoption in mind, USDtb is designed to meet the highest standards for security and regulatory compliance.
* **USDe:**USDe is a synthetic dollar created by Ethena Labs. Unlike assets backed by fiat currency, USDe maintains its dollar peg using a "delta-neutral" strategy. This involves holding an asset (like Ether or Bitcoin) and taking a short position in a perpetual futures contract for the same asset.
**Rewarding Participation**
Clients can now collect rewards for holding USDtb and USDe with Anchorage Digital. With this addition, institutions gain more flexibility and confidence in their treasury management, particularly with USDtb.
*“Anchorage Digital’s integration of minting, redemption, and rewards marks a major milestone for USDtb adoption. Together, we’re proving that stablecoins can deliver both rewards and regulatory integrity, a powerful combination that will define the next era of participation in digital assets.”
****- Guy Young, Founder, Ethena Labs
***
**Benefits for Anchorage Digital Clients**
* **Streamlined access to USDtb:**Access the first federally regulated stablecoin, which has set the standard for institutional-grade stablecoins.**‍**
* **Rewards on idle holdings\*:**Collect rewards for holding USDtb and USDe. No staking or lockups are required, so you can access your funds when you need them. Benefit from upside without compromising liquidity.**‍**
* **Enhanced capital efficiency:**Unlock new strategies by using your USDtb and USDe as collateral on the Anchorage Digital platform.
**How to Get Started**
Clients can mint, redeem, and trade**USDtb**(for other digital assets or USD) directly on the Anchorage Digital platform or via our trading desk. Clients can also**acces

# Anchorage Digital Partners with OSL to Issue First Federally Regulated US Stablecoin
Anchorage Digital partners with OSL Group to launch USDGO, marking the first stablecoin issued through a US federally regulated crypto bank framework.
![Ibrahim Ajibade](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/6442d24e28304a440d61e6b601988b5e?s=36&d=mm&r=g)By[Ibrahim Ajibade](https://www.coinspeaker.com/author/ibrahimajibade/)![Marco T. Lanz](https://www.coinspeaker.com/wp-content/themes/cs/images/team/marco-lanz-profile-photo-01.jpg)Editor[Marco T. Lanz](https://www.coinspeaker.com/author/marco-lanz/)Dec 11, 2025 at 09:32 PMUpdatedDec 11, 2025 at 09:35 PM3 mins read
![Anchorage Digital Partners with OSL to Issue First Federally Regulated US Stablecoin](https://www.coinspeaker.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/stock-image_optimized-3-1.jpg)
Share this article

* **Copy Link
### Key Notes
* Anchorage Digital will issue USDGO under federal supervision, establishing a regulated framework for stablecoin operations.
* The partnership enables 1:1 backing with liquid assets and US Treasuries through compliant custody systems.
* USDGO targets cross-border business settlements with faster transactions and programmable payment infrastructure.
Anchorage Digital, the first and only federally chartered crypto bank in the United States, will serve as the US regulated issuer for USDGO, the OSL-branded US dollar-backed stablecoin. The partnership brings together two major institutional players as the industry moves toward compliant global payment infrastructure and greater regulatory certainty for dollar-backed digital assets.
OSL Group, Asia’s leading stablecoin trading and payment platform, will launch USDGO through Anchorage Digital Bank’s federally supervised issuance framework. This makes USDGO the first stablecoin to be issued directly through a US federally regulated crypto bank, reinforcing Anchorage Digital’s positioning as a core infrastructure provider for on-shoring global stablecoin operations.
The bank’s regulated environment includes end-to-end issuance across any blockchain, strict AML and KYC standards, and asset protections aligned with US banking supervision.
Related article:[UK Financial Conduct Authority Says Supporting Stablecoins ‘a Priority’ for 2026](https://www.coinspeaker.com/uk-fca-stablecoin-payments-priority-2026/)
[According to the press release](https://www.anchorage.com/insights/anchorage-digital-serve-issuer-osls-new-us-regulated-stablecoin-usdgo), the partnership will allow USDGO to be backed 1:1 by high-quality liquid assets and US Treasuries, while benefiting from Anchorage Digital’s custody and compliance systems. editorial.headline: Anchorage Digital Onboards Western Union and Tether to Solana
editorial.standfirst: The federally chartered crypto bank confirms major stablecoin issuances for Western Union and Tether, signaling a massive migration of M2 money supply to Solana. With the Genius Act providing regulatory clarity, institutional capital is moving from experimentation to full-scale deployment on high-throughput rails.
key_takeaways: **Western Union** will launch its **USDPT** stablecoin primarily on Solana, selecting the chain for its institutional-grade settlement capabilities.
**Tether** is on-shoring liquidity via **USAT**, a new compliant stablecoin issued directly under Anchorage Digital’s federal banking charter.
**Ethena** will deploy **USDTb**—backed by **BlackRock’s BUIDL** fund—on Solana within two weeks to collateralize **Jupiter’s JupUSD**.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The passage of the Genius Act has fundamentally altered the institutional landscape, converting regulatory ambiguity into a competitive race for on-chain dominance. **Anchorage Digital**, leveraging its status as the only federally regulated stablecoin issuer, is aggressively capturing this shift by bridging legacy finance and decentralized rails. The immediate result is a bifurcated strategy: on-shoring offshore giants like **Tether** through the new **USAT** issuance, while simultaneously migrating traditional payment rails like **Western Union** onto **Solana**. This is no longer about arbitrage; it is about the systematic migration of the M2 money supply onto blockchain infrastructure.

**Western Union**’s decision to issue **USDPT** specifically on **Solana** marks a critical inflection point for the network’s institutional thesis. Moving beyond theoretical throughput, the payment giant cited the chain's programmable privacy and settlement finality as decisive factors. This deployment validates the network not just as a retail casino, but as a purpose-built rail for global settlement. The implication is that legacy fintechs are bypassing private blockchains entirely, opting instead for public, high-performance ledgers that can handle the velocity of global remittances without the friction of the correspondent banking system.

The composability of these regulated assets is creating a new yield stack on-chain. **Ethena** is set to launch **USDTb** on Solana within weeks, backed directly by **BlackRock’s BUIDL** fund. This creates a direct pipeline from short-term U.S. Treasuries to DeFi liquidity venues like **Jupiter**, where **USDTb** will back **JupUSD**. This integration effectively allows decentralized exchanges to inherit the credit quality of the world's largest asset manager while retaining the utility of a permissionless token. 

Looking ahead, the market structure is pivoting toward a multi-currency future. As sovereign states recognize that full dollarization threatens monetary sovereignty, **Anchorage Digital** anticipates a surge in non-USD stablecoins. This will likely move the global FX market—plagued by T+2 settlement cycles and counterparty risk—onto the blockchain. By enabling atomic swaps between regulated FX pairs, the industry is poised to eliminate settlement risk entirely, potentially driving on-chain stablecoin liquidity on **Solana** into the trillions by late 2026.
